- Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool: Wikipedia is a reliable source for biographical information, and this tool can help quickly access detailed pages about the authors, which might include their ethnic backgrounds.

Additional considerations:
- Verify the credibility of sources and cross-reference information from multiple reliable sources.
- Be aware that the information might not explicitly state their ethnic backgrounds and require inference from other details.
</answer>

result:
M. P. Shiel was of West Indian descent, while Helen Dunmore was not.

Matthew Phipps Shiel was born on the West Indian island of Montserrat in 1865. He was of mixed-race descent, with his mother being a mulatto, and his father having a partly Irish background and possibly also being of mixed race[1](https://1890s.ca/shiel_bio/), [2](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/biography/m-p-shiel), [3](https://1890s.ca/wp-content/uploads/shiel_bio.pdf), [4](http://gadetection.pbworks.com/w/page/7931508/Shiel%2C%20MP). Shiel is recognized as a notable West Indian author and a prolific British writer of West Indian descent[2](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/biography/m-p-shiel), [5](https://www.worldswithoutend.com/author.asp?ID=2489).

Helen Dunmore was born in Beverley, Yorkshire, England, in 1952. Her parents were Betty (née Smith) and Maurice Dunmore.[6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helen_Dunmore), [7](https://www.theguardian.com/books/2017/jun/05/helen-dunmore-obituary) She studied English at the University of York and taught in Finland before embarking on her career as a poet, novelist, and short story writer.[8](http://famouspoetsandpoems.com/poets/helen_dunmore/biography), [6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helen_Dunmore), [9](https://www.helendunmore.com/about.asp) Her origins and ethnic background are consistently described as British, with no indication of West Indian heritage.
